name: Yosemite National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for granite cliffs, waterfalls, and giant sequoias. Home to El Capitan and Half Dome.

name: Sequoia National Park
description: Home to the world's largest trees including General Sherman, and the High Sierra including Mount Whitney.

name: Kings Canyon National Park
description: Features one of North America's deepest canyons and towering groves of giant sequoias.

name: Death Valley National Park
description: Hottest, driest, and lowest national park with extraordinary desert landscapes and extreme geology.

name: Joshua Tree National Park
description: Where two distinct desert ecosystems meet, featuring iconic Joshua trees and world-class rock climbing.

name: Redwood National and State Parks
description: Home to the world's tallest trees, ancient coast redwoods reaching over 350 feet high.

name: Lassen Volcanic National Park
description: One of the few areas in the world with all four types of volcanoes and active geothermal features.

name: Channel Islands National Park
description: Often called the 'Galapagos of North America' with unique island ecosystems and marine life.

name: Pinnacles National Park
description: Unique volcanic rock formations, talus caves, and a critical habitat for California condors.

name: Rocky Mountain National Park
description: Heart of the Colorado Rockies with over 60 peaks above 12,000 feet and stunning alpine scenery.

name: Mesa Verde National Park
description: Preserves over 5,000 ancient Ancestral Puebloan archaeological sites including spectacular cliff dwellings.

name: Great Sand Dunes National Park and Preserve
description: North America's tallest sand dunes against the backdrop of the Rocky Mountains.

name: Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park
description: One of the steepest and most dramatic canyons in North America with 2,000-foot sheer walls.

name: Everglades National Park
description: Largest tropical wilderness in the United States and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Biscayne National Park
description: 95% underwater park protecting coral reefs, islands, and shipwrecks in Biscayne Bay.

name: Dry Tortugas National Park
description: Remote island park with historic Fort Jefferson and exceptional snorkeling and diving.

name: Grand Canyon National Park
description: One of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World, carved by the Colorado River over millions of years.

name: Saguaro National Park
description: Protects the iconic saguaro cactus and Sonoran Desert landscape in two districts flanking Tucson.

name: Petrified Forest National Park
description: One of the world's largest concentrations of petrified wood plus badlands and ancient pueblos.

name: Zion National Park
description: Spectacular red rock canyon with famous Angels Landing trail and stunning slot canyons.

name: Bryce Canyon National Park
description: Famous for its unique geology of colorful hoodoos - irregular spire-shaped rock formations.

name: Arches National Park
description: Contains over 2,000 natural stone arches, the highest concentration in the world.

name: Canyonlands National Park
description: Vast landscape carved by the Colorado and Green Rivers into countless canyons, mesas, and buttes.

name: Capitol Reef National Park
description: Features a 100-mile long wrinkle in the earth's crust known as the Waterpocket Fold.

name: Olympic National Park
description: Three distinct ecosystems: glacier-capped mountains, old-growth temperate rainforests, and wild Pacific coastline.

name: Mount Rainier National Park
description: Iconic 14,410-foot active volcano surrounded by wildflower meadows and ancient forests.

name: North Cascades National Park
description: American Alps with over 300 glaciers, jagged peaks, and pristine wilderness.

name: Denali National Park and Preserve
description: Home to North America's tallest peak (20,310 ft) and vast wilderness with abundant wildlife.

name: Glacier Bay National Park and Preserve
description: Spectacular tidewater glaciers, fjords, and marine wildlife in Southeast Alaska.

name: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park and Preserve
description: Largest national park - six times the size of Yellowstone with massive glaciers and peaks.

name: Kenai Fjords National Park
description: Where mountains meet the sea with massive glaciers, deep fjords, and abundant marine life.

name: Katmai National Park and Preserve
description: Famous for brown bears fishing for salmon at Brooks Falls and the Valley of Ten Thousand Smokes.

name: Lake Clark National Park and Preserve
description: Where tundra, mountains, glaciers, coastline, and two active volcanoes converge.

name: Gates of the Arctic National Park and Preserve
description: Entirely north of the Arctic Circle - America's northernmost and most remote national park.

name: Kobuk Valley National Park
description: Features Arctic sand dunes and the caribou migration through boreal forest and tundra.

name: Yellowstone National Park
description: World's first national park with over 10,000 hydrothermal features including the famous Old Faithful geyser.

name: Grand Teton National Park
description: Dramatic mountain peaks rising abruptly from the valley floor creating one of America's most iconic landscapes.

name: Glacier National Park
description: Crown of the Continent with 26 glaciers, pristine lakes, and the famous Going-to-the-Sun Road.

name: Crater Lake National Park
description: America's deepest lake (1,943 ft) formed in the caldera of an ancient volcano with incredibly blue water.

name: Great Basin National Park
description: Features ancient bristlecone pines, Lehman Caves, and Nevada's only glacier.

name: Big Bend National Park
description: Remote desert park along the Rio Grande with mountains, canyons, and the Chihuahuan Desert.

name: Guadalupe Mountains National Park
description: Contains Guadalupe Peak (highest point in Texas) and world's most extensive Permian fossil reef.

name: Carlsbad Caverns National Park
description: Over 119 caves formed in limestone with spectacular formations and massive underground chambers.

name: White Sands National Park
description: World's largest gypsum dune field with brilliant white sand dunes in the Tularosa Basin.

name: Badlands National Park
description: Dramatic landscapes of eroded buttes, pinnacles, and spires along with the nation's richest fossil beds.

name: Wind Cave National Park
description: One of the world's longest and most complex caves with rare boxwork formations and mixed-grass prairie above.

name: Theodore Roosevelt National Park
description: Badlands where Theodore Roosevelt ranched and developed his conservation ethic, now protecting wildlife and scenic landscapes.

name: Haleakalā National Park
description: Massive shield volcano crater on Maui with otherworldly landscapes and rare silversword plants.

name: Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park
description: Home to two of the world's most active volcanoes - Kīlauea and Mauna Loa.

name: Isle Royale National Park
description: Remote Lake Superior island wilderness accessible only by boat or seaplane, known for wolves and moose.

name: Voyageurs National Park
description: Water-based park named for French-Canadian fur traders, with interconnected waterways and northern forests.

name: Hot Springs National Park
description: Historic bathhouse row and natural thermal springs in an urban setting - America's smallest national park.

name: Mammoth Cave National Park
description: World's longest known cave system with over 400 miles explored and more being discovered.

name: Great Smoky Mountains National Park
description: America's most visited national park with ancient mountains, diverse wildlife, and rich cultural history.

name: Shenandoah National Park
description: 105-mile Skyline Drive through the Blue Ridge Mountains with cascading waterfalls and panoramic views.

name: Congaree National Park
description: Preserves the largest intact expanse of old growth bottomland hardwood forest in the Southeast.

name: Acadia National Park
description: Rugged Atlantic coastline, granite peaks, and pristine forests on Mount Desert Island.

name: Gateway Arch National Park
description: Iconic 630-foot arch symbolizing westward expansion of the United States in downtown St. Louis.

name: Indiana Dunes National Park
description: 15 miles of Lake Michigan shoreline with towering sand dunes and diverse ecosystems.

name: Cuyahoga Valley National Park
description: Recreational oasis along the Cuyahoga River between Cleveland and Akron with waterfalls and historic sites.

name: New River Gorge National Park and Preserve
description: Ancient river carved spectacular gorge with world-class whitewater rafting and rock climbing.

name: National Park of American Samoa
description: Tropical paradise preserving coral reefs, rainforests, and Samoan culture in the South Pacific.

name: Virgin Islands National Park
description: Covers over half of St. John island with pristine beaches, coral reefs, and historic plantation ruins.
